$description: JetStream stream declarations for the i3x v3 stack — the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for stream names, subject filters and the edge→hub source topology. Consumed by: (1) scripts/provision-jetstream.sh in the connector/edge repos (idempotent create/update of streams + sources), (2) nr-codegen (resolves which stream a transactional/telemetry NR-node publishes into — see services/nr-codegen/src/streams.ts), (3) the nats-bridge consumer config. No stream name is hardcoded in code — it is resolved against this file. CAPT-V3-HUB-EVENT-PATH.
$topology: Two JetStream tiers. EDGE tier: one NATS per Edge-IPC, JetStream domain edge_<ipc>. HUB tier: central NATS on .150, default JetStream domain. The hub does NOT capture factory.*/cpp.*/aggregate.* by core-NATS leaf propagation alone for the durable path — it SOURCES the per-edge EDGE_EXPORT stream cross-domain ($JS.edge_<ipc>.API). A transactional NR-node publishes durably into its LOCAL edge EDGE_EXPORT stream (honest ack in the edge domain); the hub FACTORY stream pulls it via the declared source. This is why a transactional publish uses expectStream=EDGE_EXPORT, not FACTORY: the edge cannot address the domain-less hub JetStream over the leaf.

edge.$description: Streams provisioned on every Edge-IPC NATS (JetStream domain edge_<ipc>). nr-codegen resolves the publish-target stream for each NR JetStream node against this list by matching the node's subject against subjectsCapture.

streams.name: EDGE_EXPORT
streams.$description: Durable export buffer on the edge. Every message the edge must hand to the Hub durably lands here first: Class-A' aggregates (aggregate.*), Class-B cycle snapshots (cpp.*) and Class-C transactional events (factory.* 4-6 segs). The Hub FACTORY stream sources this stream cross-domain. A transactional NR-node (i3x-jetstream-transactional-out) publishes here with expectStream=EDGE_EXPORT — the ack is honest because this stream really captures the subject; on a real failure the NR outbox row stays and the retry-loop owns it.
streams.$tunings: 500 MiB / 7 days on the edge — sized to one IPC's export backlog if the leaf is down for a week.

hub.$description: Streams provisioned on the central Hub NATS (.150, default JetStream domain).

streams.name: FACTORY
streams.$description: Central durable telemetry/event stream. Fed two ways: (1) direct subjectsCapture for any producer connected straight to the hub, (2) cross-domain SOURCES that pull each edge's EDGE_EXPORT stream. nats-bridge + cpp-vault consume FACTORY.

sources.name: EDGE_EXPORT
sources.$description: Cross-domain source of every Edge-IPC EDGE_EXPORT stream. provision-jetstream.sh expands one source entry per registered edge domain (edge_<ipc>) from the fleet inventory, each with api=$JS.edge_<ipc>.API. filterSubjects scopes WHICH subjects the hub pulls — all three telemetry-world roots so Class-C events reach the bridge events-writer.

streams.$tunings: 5 GiB / 7 days — Hot-Set sized to a typical week. Adjust per-customer in deploy override.

streams.name: UNS_ALERTS
streams.$description: Maintenance + quality alerts. Persistent because consumers (operator dashboard, escalation rules) must never miss one. Workqueue retention so each alert delivered once across competing consumers.

streams.name: UNS_ACTIONS
streams.$description: Plant→Edge action requests (request/reply pattern). Consumers ack within configurable deadline; unacked = redelivered until expiry.

streams.name: ERP_ENTITIES
streams.$description: Site-scoped ERP/QMS entity stream — articles, orders, customers, inspection lots etc. Lower volume than UNS_EVENTS, longer retention because some entity-types update rarely.
streams.$tunings: 1 GiB / 1 year — entities matter long-term for KG context.

deliveryClassRouting.$description: Maps an SMProfile attribute's delivery class to the JetStream stream a generated NR-node publishes into. nr-codegen reads THIS — there is no expect-stream literal in generate.ts. The stream named here must exist in tiers.edge.streams (the NR-node always publishes into its own edge domain; the hub pulls via the declared source).

transactional.$description: delivery=transactional (Class-B cycle snapshots, Class-C events). Outbox-backed durable publish.

telemetry.$description: delivery=telemetry, scope=hub (Class-A' aggregates). Fire-and-forget pub-ack. scope=edge telemetry is never published — it only lands in the Edge-Timescale.

consumers.$description: Consumer-templates — concrete consumers are created by the connector or NR-flows at runtime, but the contract here defines naming + ack-policy convention.

kg-builder.$note: kg-builder needs full history on first connect, then live tail. Durable consumer with explicit ack, max_ack_pending=1000.

historian-write.$note: Historian (TimescaleDB writer in NR) only cares about new messages — already-written history doesn't need replay.
